summary: Clear audience recording of the historic 7/6/95 show with solid playing, standout Big Boss Man and He's‑Gone jam, but a long, dull Eyes set and mixed performance notes.
review: The Schoeps audience tape captures an atmospheric snapshot of one of the Dead’s final summer shows in ’95.  Highlights include a ripping Big Boss Man, an inspired Unbroken Chain, and a soaring jam into He's‑Gone, while the first set drags and the legendary Eyes of the World feels overly long for many listeners.  Recording quality is generally good and vivid, though track labeling errors (missing drums) detract from the listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Many reviewers praise the historic setlist and decent audio, but a substantial number note dull moments, long first‑set pacing and labeling problems; overall rating falls between good show/good recording and average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and lively audience capture, crisp enough to hear vocals and instruments.  Main issues are track‑label mismatches (Drums mislabeled) and a missing drums segment.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is long and uneven, with several over‑played covers; second set picks up energy and delivers memorable jams.  The concert holds historic weight as the final performance of several classics.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed remarks – some note a decline in tone on Eyes, others celebrate his soulful singing on Stella Blue and a powerful final jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: A few reviewers praise Lesh's continued creativity in the ’90s, suggesting he kept the band expansive.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince is referenced disparagingly as “Wince” with a preference for Donna; another reviewer calls Vince "the man" of that era.
band_member_comments.Drums: One review points out the missing drums track in the labeling and mentions long drum sections in the second set.
